name: AML Disease Model
remarks: Structured AML knowledge model containing disease subtypes, genomic methods, diagnostic assays,
treatment options, conditions, observations, and quantitative measurements with ontology mappings.
ICD-10-CM:C92.00   - Acute myeloblastic leukemia, not having achieved remission
WHO-HAEM5  - WHO Classification of Haematolymphoid Tumours, 5th edition
ELN-2022   - European LeukemiaNet genetic risk classification
- AML with RUNX1::RUNX1T1 fusion (NCIT:C129785): t(8;21)(q22;q22.1), RUNX1::RUNX1T1 fusion
- AML with CBFB::MYH11 fusion (NCIT:C37214): inv(16)(p13.1q22) or t(16;16), CBFB::MYH11 fusion
- Acute promyelocytic leukemia (NCIT:C3439): PML::RARA fusion, t(15;17)(q24.1;q21.2)
- AML with mutated NPM1 (NCIT:C129786): NPM1 pathogenic mutation
- AML with bZIP in-frame mutated CEBPA (NCIT:C168785): CEBPA bZIP in-frame mutation
- Common genes: ASXL1, BCOR, EZH2, RUNX1, SF3B1, SRSF2, STAG2, U2AF1, ZRSR2
- Conventional karyotype (NCIT:C111137): Detect balanced/unbalanced chromosomal abnormalities
- Fluorescence in situ hybridization FISH (NCIT:C17356): Rapid targeted fusion/structural variant confirmation
- RT-PCR / qPCR fusion testing (NCIT:C18189): Detect recurrent fusion transcripts and molecular monitoring
- Targeted DNA NGS panel (NCIT:C157598): Identify pathogenic SNV/indel variants for diagnosis/prognosis/therapy
Genes: FLT3, NPM1, IDH1, IDH2, TP53, RUNX1, ASXL1, DNMT3A, TET2, KIT, NRAS, KRAS
- RNA sequencing (NCIT:C116071): Fusion discovery and expression profiling
- MRD flow cytometry (NCIT:C18195): Quantify measurable residual disease using leukemia-associated immunophenotypes
Interpretation: AML is typically diagnosed at >=20% blasts, except in defined genetic entities
- FLT3 internal tandem duplication status [molecular] LOINC:85176-4
- Absolute neutrophil count [hematology] LOINC:751-8  unit: 10*3/uL  range: 1.5-8.0
Clinical use: Infection risk stratification and chemotherapy supportive care
- 7+3 induction chemotherapy (NCIT:C63446): Cytarabine continuous infusion x7 days + Anthracycline x3 days
- Venetoclax + hypomethylating agent: Venetoclax + Azacitidine or decitabine (older/unfit patients)
- Tumor lysis prophylaxis: Allopurinol, Rasburicase, Aggressive hydration
- Transfusion support: Packed red blood cells, Platelets
- Favorable: NPM1-mutated without FLT3-ITD high burden; Core-binding factor AML
- Intermediate: Genetic profiles not in favorable or adverse
- Adverse: TP53 mutation; Complex karyotype; Certain myelodysplasia-related gene mutations
